_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: SOSMA Renewal Cycles Operate With Minimal Public Debate; Section 4(5) Requires It Every 5 Years
context: Section 4(5) of SOSMA 2012 requires Parliament to renew the preventive-detention provisions every 5 years. Renewal motions produce shorter debates than the law's original passage. Civil society has called for substantive reform alongside renewal.

cards.text: SOSMA's 5-year renewal cycle produces a recurring legislative ritual: short debate, narrow attendance, no substantive review.
cards.sub: Section 4(5) requires renewal every 5 years. The 2012 original received hours of debate; renewals have been substantially shorter.

cards.h: SOSMA Section 4(5) requires parliamentary renewal of the preventive-detention provisions every 5 years.
cards.s: The renewal mechanism is procedurally simple: a motion put to a vote, with debate time allocated by the Speaker and government business managers.
cards.d: SOSMA Section 4(5) requires parliamentary renewal of the preventive-detention provisions every 5 years. The renewal mechanism is procedurally simple: a motion put to a vote, with debate time allocated by the Speaker and government business managers.

cards.h: Suaram's data on SOSMA arrests (6,892 between 2018 and 2023, ~28% released without charge) is the strongest civil-society evidence on how the law actually operates.
cards.s: Much of that data is not part of the formal renewal-debate record because it post-dates the original 2012 passage.
cards.d: Suaram's data on SOSMA arrests (6,892 between 2018 and 2023, ~28% released without charge) is the strongest civil-society evidence on how the law actually operates. Much of that data is not part of the formal renewal-debate record because it post-dates the original 2012 passage.

cards.h: Civil-society petitions have been submitted at multiple renewal moments, but renewal procedure does not require parliamentary acknowledgement of public submissions.
cards.s: The Bar Council, Suaram, Lawyers for Liberty and others have all documented their interventions in successive renewal cycles.
cards.d: Civil-society petitions have been submitted at multiple renewal moments, but renewal procedure does not require parliamentary acknowledgement of public submissions. The Bar Council, Suaram, Lawyers for Liberty and others have all documented their interventions in successive renewal cycles.

cards.text: The story is not how short any single renewal debate was. It is that the renewal mechanism itself is not designed to revisit whether the law should still exist.
Like renewing an annual licence with a tick-box form: the paperwork advances, the underlying decision is never re-examined.
cards.sub: Renewal procedure is not the same as substantive review.

cards.text: A law permitting detention without trial deserves substantive review at every renewal, anchored on Suaram's arrest and release-without-charge data.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
